stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • In 2007, geologist Marjorie Chan studied the differences in age between the layered sandstone formations of the Colorado Plateau and other regional rock units.
  • Her team dated all sandstone formations within a 100-kilometer radius of Moab, Utah.
  • The Wingate Sandstone is roughly 200 million years old, which classifies it as Early Jurassic.
  • 32 of the 41 formations in the study were classified as Permian or Triassic, with ages greater than 220 million years (older than the Wingate Sandstone).
  • 3 of the 41 formations in the study were classified as Middle or Late Jurassic, with ages less than 180 million years (younger than the Wingate Sandstone).
stem: The student wants to emphasize how young the Wingate Sandstone is relative to nearby formations. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Geologist Marjorie Chan determined that the Wingate Sandstone, at roughly 200 million years old, is Early Jurassic, and several other formations within a 100-kilometer range are Middle or Late Jurassic.
Of the 41 formations within 100 kilometers of Moab, Utah, 32 are classified as Permian or Triassic, with ages greater than 220 million years.
At roughly 200 million years old, which classifies it as Early Jurassic, the Wingate Sandstone is younger than most but not all of the formations within 100 kilometers of Moab, Utah.

While most of the formations within 100 kilometers of Moab, Utah, are classified as Permian, Triassic, Middle Jurassic, or Late Jurassic, the Wingate Sandstone is classified as Early Jurassic due to its age of roughly 200 million years.

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. Noting that the Wingate Sandstone (roughly 200 million years old) is younger than most formations within 100 kilometers of Moab, Utah, the sentence emphasizes how young the Wingate Sandstone is relative to nearby formations.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence explains that Chan determined the classifications for the Wingate Sandstone and several other formations; it does not emphasize how young it is relative to nearby formations. Choice B is incorrect. The sentence explains that the majority of nearby formations are classified as Permian or Triassic; it does not indicate the Wingate Sandstone's age or emphasize how young it is. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence indicates that the Wingate Sandstone is classified differently than most nearby formations, it does not emphasize how young it is relative to those formations.
